title: mums2
text: begin analyze data, process readable data.frame object can viewed transformed. load data, need run two functions: import_all_data(), ms2_ms1_compare(). import_all_data() creates object reflecting MS1 data, ms2_ms1_compare()assigns MS2 spectra MS1 data. point, may need filter noise transform certain parameters can properly analyze data. accommodate issues, two functions: filter_peak_table(), change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute(). filter_peak_table() allows remove low-quality features change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute() allows transform retention time minutes seconds. allows ensure retention time MS1 data matches MS2 data. explain function detail illustrate go pipeline.

title: mums2
text: import_all_data() function takes two files one describing peak data (.e., peak_table) describing metadata (.e., metadata) converts mpactr object. peak_table argument takes path file describing peak table data. can specify format peak table data using format argument: Metaboscape, Progenesis, None. value given metadata argument path CSV-formatted file provides information samples. order metadata valid, needs following columns: “injection”, “sample_code”, “biological_group”. values “injection” column match sample injection columns inside peak_table. “sample_code” ID technical replicates. Finally, “biological_group” ID biological replicate groups. need deeper understanding format objects given peak_table metadata arguments , take look mpactr’s getting started page.
code: data <- import_all_data(   peak_table = mums2_example("boryillus_peaktable.csv"),    metadata = mums2_example("boryillus_metadata.csv"),    format = "Progenesis")

title: mums2
text: expected format Progenesis peak table. contains samples columns features rows. feature intensities expected un-normalized.

what: Convert retention time to “rt in minutes” or “rt in seconds”
title: mums2
text: Sometimes MS2 file report retention time minutes MS1 file report seconds. mismatch cause incorrect peak matching MS1 MS2 data. change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute() function allows users synthesize data different units. aware function modifies mpactr object place. title: mums2
text: can use .mgf/.mzxml/.mzml-formatted file match MS2 spectra MS1 peaks. ms2_ms1_compare() function reads list mgf files matches MS1 feature based mass--charge ratio retention time tolerance. multiple matches, function select MS2 spectra highest intensity. Keep mind MS2 spectra files large, can anywhere 1 MB 100 GB. Therefore, depending big file , might take moments function complete. ms2_ms1_compare() generates list data two data frames (ms1_data, ms2_matches), list (peak_data), character vector (“samples”): ms2_matches data.frame object contains five columns: mz, rt, ms1_compound_id, spectra_index, ms2_spectrum_id. mz rt reported MS2 file mass--charge ratio retention time, respectively. ms1_compound_id compound id imported MS1 peak_table. spectra_index matches MS2 data MS2 spectrum. Finally, ms2_spectrum_id generated name represent MS2 spectra (combination mz rt). necessary properly compare compounds. Since compounds similar structures expected similar MS2 patterns, can use algorithmic techniques compute similarity two spectra. allows us generate annotations cluster similar spectra together. ms1_data data.frame object containing data created running filtering steps mpactr. peak_data list object generated ms2_ms1_compare(). peak_data collection MS2 peak list. peak list collection fragment ions, value represent intensity mass-charge ratio. samples character vector named listing groups/samples contained peak_table file.

title: Annotate LC-MS/MS features — annotate_ms2
text: Annotate query LC-MS/MS features mass_data object given reference list. annotate_ms2() allows annotation mass spectrometry features. Similarity query reference level 2 spectra determined via spectral scoring methods. Currently scoring methods "gnps" "spectral_entropy" supported. scoring method specified score_params argument. score_params list parameters chosen scoring method. Parameters "gnps" "spectral_entropy" can created functions modified_cosine_params() spec_entropy_params(), respectively. using hmdb reference database, database contain precursormz please ensure expand ppm account difference.

text: mass_data object generated ms2_ms1_compare(). reference reference database generated read_msp() function. currently support msp files. scoring_params Parameters scoring method applied. can either modified_cosine_params() spec_entropy_params(). ppm Parts per million. MS2 scans difference ppm less equal value scored. min_score Similarity score threshold determine match annotation. Comparisons scores value reported. chemical_min_score data2 cluster_data cluster object generated cluster_data(). Can also remain NULL wish annotate data without omu information. min_peaks minimum number peaks need present compare ms2 spectra. number_of_threads number threads wish use calculation. Defaults number threads computer.

title: create community matrix — create_community_matrix
text: cluster_object result cluster_data() function. data <- import_all_data(peak_table = mums2::mums2_example("botryllus_pt_small.csv"), metadata = mums2::mums2_example("boryillus_metadata.csv"), format = "None") filtered_data <- data |> filter_peak_table(filter_mispicked_ions_params()) |> filter_peak_table(filter_cv_params(cv_threshold = 0.2)) |> filter_peak_table(filter_group_params(group_threshold = 0.1, "Blanks")) |> filter_peak_table(filter_insource_ions_params()) change_rt_to_seconds_or_minute(filtered_data, "minutes") matched_data <- ms2_ms1_compare(mums2_example("botryllus_v2.gnps.mgf"), filtered_data, 10, 6) dist <- dist_ms2(data = matched_data, cutoff = 0.3, precursor_thresh = 2, score_params = modified_cosine_params(0.5), min_peaks = 0) cluster_results <- cluster_data(distance_df = dist, ms2_match_data = matched_data, cutoff = 0.3, cluster_method = "opticlust") community_matrix <- create_community_matrix_object(cluster_results)

title: mums2: Microbial Ecology by Tandem Mass Spectrometry — mums2-package
text: Tools researchers can use analyze untargeted metabolomics data generated using tandem mass spectroscopy microbial communities. overall approach taken analyze metabolomics data parallels used analyze microbial communities using 16S rRNA gene sequencing data. Thus, number methods user able use generate data. Firstly, users can import Mass Spectrometry 1(MS1) data filter . Users able match Mass Spectrometry 2(MS2) data filtered (unfiltered) MS1 data. matched data users able cluster , annotate , predict de novo chemical formulas calculate alpha beta diversity. chemical formula predictions, method used; Towards de novo identification metabolites analyzing tandem mass spectra (Sebastian Böcker, Florian Rasche (2008) doi:10.1093/bioinformatics/btn270 ). similarity/dissimilarity calculations used cluster data together : Spectral entropy outperforms MS/MS dot product similarity small-molecule compound identification (Li, Y., Kind, T., Folz, J. et al. (2021) doi:10.1038/s41592-021-01331-z ) Sharing community curation mass spectrometry data Global Natural Products Social Molecular Networking (Wang, M., Carver, J., Phelan, V. et al. (2021) doi:10.1038/nbt.3597 ).

title: Entropy similarity between two MS/MS spectra — spec_entropy_params
text: ms2_tolerance_in_da MS2 peak tolerance Da, set -1 disable. Defaults 0.02. ms2_tolerance_in_ppm MS2 peak tolerance ppm, set -1 disable. Defaults -1. clean_spectra Either TRUE FALSE clean spectra prior calculating similarity. Defaults TRUE. min_mz numeric, minimum mz keep, set -1 disable. Defaults 0. max_mz numeric, maximum mz keep, set -1 disable. Defaults 1000. noise_threshold Background intensity threshold, peaks intensity < noise_threshold * max_intensity removed. Set -1 disable. Defaults 0.01. max_peak_num numeric, maximum number peaks keep score calculation. Set -1 disable. Defaults 100. weighted logical whether weighted unweighted entropy similarity calculated. Defaults TRUE.
